description: Identifies ECS Fargate services with over-provisioned task CPU or memory, missing Auto Scaling, or stale platform versions that result in unnecessary compute cost.

cost.pricing_source: AWS Fargate list price. Effective savings may differ if Compute Savings Plans or Fargate Reserved Capacity are in use — check cost_report for actual effective rates.
cost.note: Fargate charges are per vCPU-hour and per GB-hour for the provisioned task size, regardless of actual utilization. Right-sizing directly reduces the provisioned cost.

severity_rules.name: fargate_cpu_overprovisioned
severity_rules.condition: launch_type == FARGATE AND cpu_avg_pct < 20 AND cpu_p95_pct < 50 AND cpu_max_pct < 80
severity_rules.sizing_formula: ceil(cpu_p95_actual_units * 1.3) rounded up to next valid Fargate cpu_units value
severity_rules.description: Task CPU is chronically under-utilized. Fargate bills for provisioned vCPU regardless of actual use. Right-sizing to the next valid CPU tier that covers p95 × 1.3 eliminates the over-provisioned portion.

severity_rules.name: fargate_memory_overprovisioned
severity_rules.condition: launch_type == FARGATE AND memory_avg_pct < 20 AND memory_p95_pct < 50 AND memory_max_pct < 90
severity_rules.sizing_formula: ceil(memory_p95_actual_mb * 1.3) rounded up to nearest 512 MB, must be within valid range for target CPU
severity_rules.description: Task memory is chronically under-utilized. Fargate bills for provisioned GB-hours. Right-size memory to p95 × 1.3, ensuring the result is a valid Fargate memory value for the selected CPU tier.

severity_rules.name: missing_ecs_autoscaling
severity_rules.condition: desired_count is static AND no aws_appautoscaling_target referencing this service
severity_rules.description: Service runs a fixed number of tasks with no Auto Scaling policy. Static desired_count pays for idle capacity during low-traffic periods and risks under-provisioning during traffic spikes. Add Target Tracking on ECSServiceAverageCPUUtilization at 60–70%.

severity_rules.name: stale_platform_version
severity_rules.condition: platform_version != LATEST AND platform_version is pinned (e.g. '1.3.0', '1.4.0')
severity_rules.description: Fargate platform version is pinned to a specific release. Use LATEST to receive security patches, Fargate ephemeral storage improvements, and new networking features automatically.

steps: Deploy right-sized task definition to a non-production environment first.
Run load test at expected peak traffic to confirm no OOMKilled or CPU throttling events.
Deploy to production with rolling update (minimumHealthyPercent: 100, maximumPercent: 200).
Monitor for 7 days: CPUUtilization, MemoryUtilization, and OOMKilled metric / container insights events.
Roll back to previous task definition if OOMKilled events appear or CPU consistently exceeds 85%.

terraform: Require cpu and memory to be set explicitly on every aws_ecs_task_definition.
Require aws_appautoscaling_target and aws_appautoscaling_policy for every aws_ecs_service with desired_count > 1.
Set platform_version = 'LATEST' on all aws_ecs_service resources unless a specific version is required for compatibility.
Add environment, workload_pattern, and owner tags to every aws_ecs_service.
monitoring: Alert when ECSServiceAverageCPUUtilization < 15% for 7 consecutive days (sustained waste signal).
Alert when ECSServiceAverageMemoryUtilization < 15% for 7 consecutive days.
Alert on OOMKilled events in Container Insights — indicates memory is too low.
Track p95 and max CPU/memory, not only averages.
